fixes_applied: Fix outcomes path mismatch (.monomind/ -> .monomind/data/)
Agent interactions: detect failure from result summary instead of hardcoding success=1
Episode content: strip system XML noise from stored prompts
Memory ops: wire consumer that logs stats at session-end
Routing feedback: wire reader that checks historical accuracy
Rebuilt monograph dist so agent_interactions table is created on DB open via applyMigrations
Wired taskSuccess into intelligence.feedback() instead of hardcoded true
memory-system.convergence_rationale: Both code-level fixes confirmed working. Remaining value requires accumulating real usage data over sessions, not more code changes.

fixes_applied: Same fixes as memory-system — hook handlers are the execution layer
Persist recentEdits to disk (recent-edits.jsonl) so feedback() reads edits across subprocess boundaries
Store _lastContext from getContext() so feedback records include what intelligence suggested
consolidate() now synthesizes successful outcomes with edits into auto-memory-store.json for getContext() to use
feedback() no longer clears recent-edits.jsonl — only consolidate() clears at session-end (prevents post-task from consuming session-level edits)
consolidate() falls back to session-level edits when outcomes have empty recentEdits (breaks bootstrap deadlock)
consolidate() deduplicates edit paths and caps store at 200 entries
consolidate() rotates outcomes file at 500 lines (prevents unbounded growth)
getContext() requires minimum 2 word matches (3+ char words) to reduce false positives
getContext() ranks matches by hit count then confidence (was arbitrary first-match)
Removed false 'PageRank recomputed' claim from session-handler consolidation message

verification.flaky_test: control-start.test.mjs (ENOTEMPTY temp cleanup race — pre-existing, unrelated)
verification.data_diagnosis: All 64 existing outcomes have empty recentEdits — fix A/B will resolve on next session cycle

fixes_applied: Session-end ordering: moved feedback() BEFORE consolidate() so outcomes capture recentEdits (was always empty because consolidate cleared recent-edits.jsonl first)
consolidate() path extraction: e.path || e replaced with null-safe check that filters empty-path entries instead of producing [object Object]

Test isolation: tests/routing/intelligence-cjs.test.mjs now uses CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR temp dir (was writing to production .monomind/data/), added 2 new tests (feedback captures edits, consolidate produces clean store)
Cleaned 65 polluted auto-memory-store.json entries (all contained [object Object] and fake test paths)

verification.e2e_data_flow: recordEdit -> feedback(with edits) -> consolidate(pattern) -> init(loads) -> getContext(returns match) -- CONFIRMED

intelligence-system.convergence_rationale: All three bugs in the intelligence data pipeline are fixed and verified end-to-end. Session 1 edits flow into auto-memory-store.json; Session 2 getContext returns relevant suggestions. No [object Object] pollution. No test data leaking into production files. The system now genuinely learns from successful sessions. Value increase from 3 to 7 reflects the pipeline going from broken-but-compensated to actually working.

fixes_applied: Added 94 tests across 7 test files (cosine, encoder, keyword-pre-filter, llm-fallback, capability-index, classify-prompt, route-layer)
Added vitest.config.ts for the routing package
routing-package.convergence_rationale: Package delivers genuine value: real embedding-based semantic routing (Snowflake arctic-embed-xs, ~58% top-1), 3-tier cascade (keyword->semantic->LLM fallback), production hardening (isolated worker process, centroid caching, graceful degradation). Complementary to router.cjs (hooks pipeline) -- not redundant. Route-outcome correlation is in CLI monovector module, not this package (audit label was slightly misleading). No code bugs found. Added comprehensive test suite to close the only gap.

fixes_applied: Wired @monomind/routing keyword-pre-filter bridge into route-handler.cjs: when router.cjs falls to 'coder' catch-all (confidence <= 0.8), dynamically imports @monomind/routing's DEFAULT_KEYWORD_ROUTES (30+ specialized rules) for a more specific match
Bridge uses 2 filesystem fallback paths (direct dist/ or CLI node_modules symlink), fully try/catch wrapped for graceful degradation when routing package unavailable
Added 2 new tests: enrichment fires for specialized prompts (Solidity), skipped for specific non-coder matches
routing-package.remaining_work: Semantic (embedding) routing in hooks path would require solving ESM/latency constraints — keyword bridge is the pragmatic solution

fixes_applied: Wired obsidian and canvas export formats in MCP tool (implementations existed in @monoes/monograph but were not connected)
Fixed monograph_watch_stop stub: added _activeWatchers Map to track watcher references, watch registers them, watch_stop actually stops them
Extracted shared applyPprRerank helper from duplicated code in monograph_query and monograph_ppr_rerank

verification.ppr_deduped: single applyPprRerank at line 51, used by monograph_query and monograph_ppr_rerank

monograph.convergence_rationale: Monograph is the highest-value package in monomind (8/10 value). 47 MCP tools with 46 real implementations. Graph-powered capabilities (impact analysis, PPR reranking, community detection, dead code, Cypher queries) genuinely transcend grep/find. Three targeted honesty-gap fixes applied: export wiring, watch_stop stub, PPR dedup. Only remaining gap is monograph_embed (deprecated no-op, intentional). No further code changes needed -- remaining value comes from usage.

fixes_applied: matchSkills now counts distinct keyword hits per skill (was binary 1.0 for all matches)
Skills registry gains _terms arrays for multi-keyword scoring
Auto-activation threshold lowered from score>=3 (unreachable) to score>=2 (fires on 2+ keyword hits)
Skills regex switched to global flag for consistent matching

verification.coder_catchall_safe: last in DEV_PRIORITY — only fires as fallback

router-system.convergence_rationale: Scoring fix from iteration 1 confirmed working. Auto-activation fires on 2+ keyword hits. No bugs found. Remaining improvements are additive features (more skills in registry), not fixes.

verification.http_server_works: delegates to @monomind/mcp with CLI tool registration
security_hardening: loopback gate for HTTP/WS (requires MONOMIND_MCP_ALLOW_REMOTE=1 + 32-char token)

mcp-server.convergence_rationale: MCP server is the highest-value integration point in monomind -- it bridges all ~300+ tools to Claude Code. The stdio handler (primary path) is well-implemented with comprehensive security hardening. The @monomind/mcp framework package provides HTTP/WS transport with its own session management, rate limiting, and tool registry. 65 tests pass. No bugs found. Protocol version '2024-11-05' is correct for Claude Code's MCP client. The dual-architecture (direct stdio handler + framework for HTTP/WS) is pragmatic engineering.

guidance-package.convergence_rationale: Guidance is a substantial, well-engineered governance framework (21k lines, 31 modules, 1346 tests at 88.6% coverage). It provides real value: CLAUDE.md compilation into policy bundles, enforcement gates for destructive ops and secrets, run ledgers with evaluators, trust systems, proof chains, adversarial defenses, and CLAUDE.md quality analysis/optimization. The CLI exposes 7 subcommands (compile, retrieve, gates, status, optimize, ab-test, setup) and 5 MCP guidance tools. The init wizard wires gates into hooks. The package has NO runtime consumers outside the CLI guidance command (no other package imports it), meaning it is opt-in tooling rather than a core runtime dependency. This is appropriate -- it is a governance control plane meant to be invoked explicitly. No bugs found. The only gaps are low test coverage on headless.ts and wasm-kernel.ts, which are infrastructure modules not yet fully implemented. No code changes needed.

fixes_applied: Wired GuidanceHookProvider into session-restore-handler.cjs: after initDefaultWorkers(), imports @monomind/guidance, compiles CLAUDE.md + CLAUDE.local.md, loads shards into retriever, registers 5 hooks on defaultRegistry (PreCommand, PreToolUse, PreEdit, PreTask, PostTask)
Activates shard retrieval at PreTask (task-relevant CLAUDE.md rules injected per task), run ledger tracking at PostTask, and diff-size gate at PreEdit — all previously dead code
Fixed var fs shadowing bug (var fs inside try block shadowed const fs at file top, breaking later fs.existsSync calls)

verification.graceful_degradation: all try/catch wrapped — non-fatal if @monomind/guidance unavailable

guidance-package.convergence_rationale: GuidanceHookProvider was fully implemented but never instantiated at runtime — shard retrieval, ledger tracking, and diff-size gate were dead code. Now wired into session-restore-handler.cjs alongside initDefaultWorkers(). CLAUDE.md is compiled once per session, shards loaded into retriever, and 5 hooks registered. Each task gets relevant CLAUDE.md rules injected (not the whole doc). Run events tracked for future optimization cycles. Value 7->8.

capabilities: CDP WebSocket client with command correlation
Headless/headed mode with auto-switch on login/CAPTCHA
Accessibility tree snapshots with ref-based element model
Device emulation, geolocation, offline, color scheme
CPU profiling, Chrome tracing, Web Vitals
PDF generation, HAR recording, console capture
Action builder analyzer for AI-driven automation

monobrowse.convergence_rationale: Monobrowse is a well-implemented, lightweight CDP browser automation engine with genuine differentiators: ref-based accessibility element model optimized for AI agents, automatic login/CAPTCHA detection with headed fallback, session persistence, and zero binary downloads (uses system Chrome). 43 source files across 7020 lines with only 2 runtime dependencies (ws, commander). Clean build, comprehensive resource bounding, proper security validation. Used as the engine for monomind browse CLI command. 348 lines of tests in the CLI package. No bugs found. No code changes needed.

verification.agent_store_persistence: .monomind/agents/store.json with 50MB cap and 500 terminated agent pruning
verification.consensus_coordination: swarm coordinate command shows v1 15-agent mesh plan and calls MCP swarm_init

swarm-agents.convergence_rationale: The agents module is lean but well-integrated. The src/agents/ directory contains only registry-builder.ts (239 lines), which scans .claude/agents/*.md files (144 definitions), parses YAML frontmatter, and produces a unified registry JSON. This feeds into startup (index.ts), doctor checks, and agent routing. The actual agent lifecycle (spawn/list/status/stop/metrics/pool/health) lives in commands/agent-lifecycle.ts and agent-ops.ts, delegating to MCP tools for state management. The swarm command (912 lines) provides init/start/status/stop/scale/coordinate with real MCP integration and disk-persisted state. The MCP agent-tools.ts provides file-backed agent persistence with proper resource bounding (50MB store cap, 500 terminated agent pruning). Consensus coordination is implemented through swarm topology selection (hierarchical, mesh, ring, star, hybrid, hierarchical-mesh) with the coordinate command showing the v1 15-agent plan. No dead or broken agent types found. The spawn command integrates with route-layer-factory for auto-routing by task description. All 880 tests pass. No honesty gaps -- the CLI honestly delegates to MCP for actual execution and clearly states when MCP is unavailable. No code changes needed.

fixes_applied: Unified swarm state storage: CLI swarm init/start/stop now read/write .monomind/swarm/swarm-state.json (MCP-canonical path) instead of .swarm/state.json — CLI and MCP tools share state
CLI swarm init writes MCP store format ({ swarms: { [id]: SwarmState } }) so swarm_shutdown can find CLI-initialized swarms
CLI swarm stop updates specific swarm entry in MCP store format instead of legacy flat file
getSwarmStatus() reads agent counts from .monomind/agents/store.json (where MCP agent_spawn actually stores records) instead of nonexistent .swarm/agents/*.json
getSwarmStatus() parses MCP store format to find latest non-terminated swarm, with legacy format fallback

verification.bug_fixed: swarm stop for CLI-initialized swarms no longer fails with 'not found' because both CLI and MCP write to same file

swarm-agents.convergence_rationale: The dual-state-path bug was the primary honesty gap — CLI wrote to .swarm/state.json while MCP wrote to .monomind/swarm/swarm-state.json, making swarm stop fail for CLI-initiated swarms and swarm status show 0 agents despite MCP having spawned them. Both paths now unified. Agent counts come from the real MCP agent store. Value 7->8.

fixes_applied: Marked gossip and CRDT strategies as 'planned -- not yet implemented' in CONSENSUS_STRATEGIES constant (hive-mind-helpers.ts)
Added explicit rejection in hive-mind_consensus handler when gossip/crdt strategy is passed (returns error with available strategies list)
Added validation in hive-mind_init handler: gossip/crdt consensus requests get a warning and default to 'byzantine' instead of echoing unimplemented strategy

Added 37 tests in tests/hive-mind/consensus.test.mjs covering vote-signer (deriveSigningKey, signVote, verifyVote, weightedTally) and audit-writer (record, verify, tamper detection, path traversal guard, swarmId filtering)

verification.gossip_crdt_honest: returns explicit 'not yet implemented' error instead of silent fallthrough
verification.init_consensus_validated: gossip/crdt default to byzantine with warning

hive-mind.convergence_rationale: BFT/Raft/Quorum real. Gossip/CRDT honestly labeled. No further improvements without real distributed IPC.

verification.detection_patterns: 25+ regex patterns for prompt injection, jailbreak, role-switching
verification.pii_detection: email, SSN, credit card, API keys, passwords
verification.output_scanning: PII leakage, prompt echo (trigram Jaccard), policy violations, contradictions
verification.context_tracking: 4-state escalation machine with cumulative scoring and idle decay
verification.self_learning: ReasoningBank trajectory recording, EMA mitigation effectiveness

monofence.convergence_rationale: Monofence is production-quality security middleware with comprehensive guardrails. 25+ detection patterns with evasion normalization, PII scanning, output analysis, multi-turn state machine, and multi-agent consensus. 91 tests pass across 8 test files at 75% statement coverage. Published to npm, deeply integrated via 6 MCP tools, CLI security scan command, and hooks auto-registration. The only coverage gap is the learning service (39%) which requires real usage data to exercise fully. No code changes needed.

fixes_applied: Added Status column to CLI commands table in CLAUDE.md
Changed '60+ agent types' to '60+ routing target definitions'
Changed hive-mind description to 'BFT/Raft/Quorum vote counting (single-process)'
Removed phantom InputValidator/PathValidator/SafeExecutor agent claims
Changed neural description to 'Pattern storage'

cli-commands.convergence_rationale: Value jumped 4→7, honesty gap 7→3 after ab50cd81 cleanup. Commands honestly labeled, most work end-to-end. In-process MCP tool registry means commands work standalone without requiring an MCP server. No further fixes needed.

fixes_applied: Wired security worker into session-start via session-restore-handler.cjs
Documented two-system architecture at top of hook-handler.cjs
Updated CLAUDE.md: split '12 workers' into daemon (12) vs hooks (11) with accurate names
Wired @monomind/hooks WorkerManager into CLI startup (index.ts) alongside initDefaultWorkers
Removed dead cleanup import from agent-tools.ts (security package has no cleanup export)

hooks-system.convergence_rationale: Value jumped 3→7, health 5→8 after WorkerManager wiring into CLI startup. 10 real workers with genuine OS/git/security introspection, 790 tests pass. Remaining honesty gap (5) is alias naming in CLAUDE.md — cosmetic, not functional.

fixes_applied: Wired validateExternalContent into memory_pattern-store and memory_pattern-search
Removed phantom WasmSandbox/DockerSandbox/register import from agent-tools.ts
Wired validateInput into MCP server tools/call dispatch

security-package.convergence_rationale: Value 2→6, honesty 9→3 after ab50cd81 cleanup + iteration 1 wiring. Validators genuinely wired at MCP boundary and memory tools. Minor remaining issues (misleading CVE remediation docs) are cosmetic.

fixes_applied: Registered sparc, monobrowse, tokens skills in router
Added coder catch-all bypass when skill match score >= 2

skills-system.convergence_rationale: Value 6→7, health 5→7. Core skills (mastermind, monolean, monodesign, monomotion) work well with real routing integration. Honesty gap (6) is about ~183 legacy command files inflating perceived scope, not broken functionality. No further code fixes warranted.

fixes_applied: Wired security-audit.json into route-handler as [SECURITY] line
Wired codebase-map.json into route-handler as [CODEBASE] line

daemon-system.convergence_rationale: Value 1→6, honesty 9→2 after ab50cd81 cleanup. Daemon genuinely daemonizes, 7 of 12 workers run real analysis against monograph DB. Workers honestly label 5 as needing external CLI. Metrics pipeline (write to .monomind/metrics/) now partially consumed by route-handler.

fixes_applied: Wired trainCommand: reads outcomes + edits, stores patterns via ReasoningBank
Renamed all neural descriptions to honest 'pattern storage/similarity search'

neural-system.convergence_rationale: Value 4→5, honesty 7→4. Working kNN similarity search with real cosine math. Partially self-aware about not being real ML (neural-core.ts disclaims 'no ML training'). Remaining gap is naming conventions (EWCConsolidator, 'neural train'), not broken functionality.

fixes_applied: Created shared cosine-similarity.ts utility in src/utils/ with canonical implementation (supports number[] and Float32Array, graceful dimension mismatch via Math.min, zero-magnitude safe)

Replaced 5 independent cosine similarity implementations: intelligence.ts ReasoningBank.cosineSim -> delegates to shared util; neural-tools.ts standalone -> import from shared util; embeddings-tools.ts standalone -> import from shared util; coherence/types.ts exported -> re-export from shared util; coherence/coherence-check.ts standalone -> import from shared util
Moved all mid-file imports to file top for proper style

verification.behavioral_change: embeddings-tools.ts epsilon (1e-15) removed in favor of exact zero check; neural-tools.ts dimension mismatch now uses Math.min instead of throwing

fixes_applied: Wired SessionEnd bridge in session-handler.cjs: fires executeHooks(HookEvent.SessionEnd) so EpisodeBinner.closeEpisode() and observability bus session.end event actually run
Wired PostEdit bridge in edit-handler.cjs: fires executeHooks(HookEvent.PostEdit) so observability bus and guidance provider receive edit events

cross-docs-honesty.convergence_rationale: Both CLAUDE.md files now accurately reflect the codebase: all 41 CLI commands listed (no phantoms), correct hook/worker counts, honest consensus strategy status, complete package inventory. AI agents reading CLAUDE.md will no longer be misled into using nonexistent commands or expecting phantom agent types. Remaining honesty gap (2) is inherent drift risk in manual docs.
